name: James Mackler
subject: What we're fighting for:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Change
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa112/18d6a294/4caf15fd/26d7f986/3191415399/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Friends -- Thank you. This team came together last month and we blew away our May fundraising goal, and I'm grateful for all you do. Being in this fight together is the only way we're going to flip this open Senate seat, change the majority, and address what needs to change in our country. **We 're fighting for change.** In the past few days it's clear just how desperately we need real and serious changes in our country. The only way to truly make progress is to listen to Tennesseans, and fight for what we need and are not currently getting from Washington. Our next senator isn't going to be able to do that if we send another rubber stamp beholden to special interests to the Senate. That's why I said I wouldn't take corporate PAC money from the beginning of this campaign. Our campaign is a grassroots-powered movement -- I can't do any of this without you. Thank you again for your continued support, for your commitment to change, and for being in this fight with me. In service, James | | | [Donate](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa112/18d6a294/4caf15fd/26d7f987/3191415399/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxMDc0MjMiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJqbWUyMDA2MDMiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jme200603?refcode=jme200603_m_other&refcode2=107423")

clean_body: Change | "" | | Friends -- Thank you. This team came together last month and we blew away our May fundraising goal, and I'm grateful for all you do. Being in this fight together is the only way we're going to flip this open Senate seat, change the majority, and address what needs to change in our country. We 're fighting for change. In the past few days it's clear just how desperately we need real and serious changes in our country. The only way to truly make progress is to listen to Tennesseans, and fight for what we need and are not currently getting from Washington. Our next senator isn't going to be able to do that if we send another rubber stamp beholden to special interests to the Senate. That's why I said I wouldn't take corporate PAC money from the beginning of this campaign. Our campaign is a grassroots-powered movement -- I can't do any of this without you. Thank you again for your continued support, for your commitment to change, and for being in this fight with me. name: Team Cotton
subject: Cotton in the New York Times: End the Riots
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
body:
Check out Tom’s latest opinion piece in the New York Times about his plan to end the violence tonight.Don't be fooled by the media's coverage of these riots. The vast majority of Americans are horrified by the anarchy and looting, and they want it to stop now. If local law enforcement needs backup, there is a long history of the military helping keep Americans safe from riots.Click here to unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42651062&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDI2ODMyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0
http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2NTEwNjIS1&kn=1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDI2ODMyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0 Peter,Don't be fooled by the media's coverage of these riots.

The vast majority of Americans are horrified by the anarchy and looting, and they want it to stop now.

If local law enforcement needs backup, there is a long history of the military helping keep Americans safe from riots.

Check out Tom’s latest opinion piece in the New York Times about his plan to end the violence tonight.

Donate to Help Tom Fight Back http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2NTEwNjIS1&kn=2&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDI2ODMyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0
- Team CottonSend In the Troops
The nation must restore order. The military stands ready. By Tom Cotton Mr. Cotton, a Republican, is a United States senator from Arkansas. This week, rioters have plunged many American cities into anarchy, recalling the widespread violence of the 1960s.

New York City suffered the worst of the riots Monday night, as Mayor Bill de Blasio stood by while Midtown Manhattan descended into lawlessness. Bands of looters roved the streets, smashing and emptying hundreds of businesses. Some even drove exotic cars; the riots were carnivals for the thrill-seeking rich as well as other criminal elements.

Outnumbered police officers, encumbered by feckless politicians, bore the brunt of the violence. In New York State, rioters ran over officers with cars on at least three occasions. In Las Vegas, an officer is in “grave” condition after being shot in the head by a rioter. In St. Louis, four police officers were shot as they attempted to disperse a mob throwing bricks and dumping gasoline; in a separate incident, a 77-year-old retired police captain was shot to death as he tried to stop looters from ransacking a pawnshop. This is “somebody’s granddaddy,” a bystander screamed at the scene.

Some elites have excused this orgy of violence in the spirit of radical chic, calling it an understandable response to the wrongful death of George Floyd. Those excuses are built on a revolting moral equivalence of rioters and looters to peaceful, law-abiding protesters. A majority who seek to protest peacefully shouldn’t be confused with bands of miscreants.

But the rioting has nothing to do with George Floyd, whose bereaved relatives have condemned violence. On the contrary, nihilist criminals are simply out for loot and the thrill of destruction, with cadres of left-wing radicals like antifa infiltrating protest marches to exploit Floyd’s death for their own anarchic purposes.

These rioters, if not subdued, not only will destroy the livelihoods of law-abiding citizens but will also take more innocent lives. Many poor communities that still bear scars from past upheavals will be set back still further.

One thing above all else will restore order to our streets: an overwhelming show of force to disperse, detain and ultimately deter lawbreakers. But local law enforcement in some cities desperately needs backup, while delusional politicians in other cities refuse to do what’s necessary to uphold the rule of law.

The pace of looting and disorder may fluctuate from night to night, but it’s past time to support local law enforcement with federal authority. Some governors have mobilized the National Guard, yet others refuse, and in some cases the rioters still outnumber the police and Guard combined. In these circumstances, the Insurrection Act authorizes the president to employ the military “or any other means” in “cases of insurrection, or obstruction to the laws.”

This venerable law, nearly as old as our republic itself, doesn’t amount to “martial law” or the end of democracy, as some excitable critics, ignorant of both the law and our history, have comically suggested. In fact, the federal government has a constitutional duty to the states to “protect each of them from domestic violence.” Throughout our history, presidents have exercised this authority on dozens of occasions to protect law-abiding citizens from disorder. Nor does it violate the Posse Comitatus Act, which constrains the military’s role in law enforcement but expressly excepts statutes such as the Insurrection Act.

For instance, during the 1950s and 1960s, Presidents Dwight Eisenhower, John Kennedy and Lyndon Johnson called out the military to disperse mobs that prevented school desegregation or threatened innocent lives and property. This happened in my own state. Gov. Orval Faubus, a racist Democrat, mobilized our National Guard in 1957 to obstruct desegregation at Little Rock Central High School. President Eisenhower federalized the Guard and called in the 101st Airborne in response. The failure to do so, he said, “would be tantamount to acquiescence in anarchy.”

More recently, President George H.W. Bush ordered the Army’s Seventh Infantry and 1,500 Marines to protect Los Angeles during race riots in 1992. He acknowledged his disgust at Rodney King’s treatment — “what I saw made me sick” — but he knew deadly rioting would only multiply the victims, of all races and from all walks of life.

Not surprisingly, public opinion is on the side of law enforcement and law and order, not insurrectionists. According to a recent poll, 58 percent of registered voters, including nearly half of Democrats and 37 percent of African-Americans, would support cities’ calling in the military to “address protests and demonstrations” that are in “response to the death of George Floyd.” That opinion may not appear often in chic salons, but widespread support for it is fact nonetheless.

The American people aren’t blind to injustices in our society, but they know that the most basic responsibility of government is to maintain public order and safety. In normal times, local law enforcement can uphold public order. But in rare moments, like ours today, more is needed, even if many politicians prefer to wring their hands while the country burns. Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

Privacy Policy http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2NTEwNjIS1&kn=4&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MDI2ODMyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0
Unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42651062&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDI2ODMyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0

clean_body: There’s no doubt about it – with our current challenges, this has been a unique primary season. Friend, There’s no doubt about it – with our current challenges, this has been a unique primary season. The primary is now behind us and we now have our slate of Republican candidates. Here’s a quick list of some of the Republicans that will join Eric on the ballot this November. For a full list of all candidates that will be on the ballot in November, check with your County Clerk: POTUS: Governor: First Congressional District Nominee: Second Congressional District Nominee: Third Congressional District Nominee: Fourth Congressional District Nominee: Fifth Congressional District Nominee: Sixth Congressional District Nominee: Seventh Congressional District Nominee: Eighth Congressional District Nominee: Ninth Congressional District Nominee: ** Voting is one of the biggest ways to make an impact in your nation, state, and community. If you didn’t vote yesterday, make sure that you’re registered to vote by October 5 so that you make your voice heard on November 3. #INThisTogether, -Your Holcomb Crew | | | | | | Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. All rights reserved. | | Eric Holcomb for Indiana, 101 W Ohio Street, Suite 2200, Indianapolis, IN 46204 | (317) 569-0709 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

If you'd rather not get all of our updates you can [click here](https://act.314actionfund.org/page/m/29bb7481/4bd7760e/5d3e5f35/77ae4b0e/1097093776/VEsH/?g=v87XQ8Qot1BdKB-_uknky5A "https://314action.org/less-email/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=naughton&utm_content=1+-+click+here&utm_campaign=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&source=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&refcode=314_EM_FR_200603_3MO-3MC-3M5xAT_1M2xOP-1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr") for only the most important emails, or you can click [here](https://act.314actionfund.org/page/m/29bb7481/4bd7760e/5d3e5f35/77ae4b0d/1097093776/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZQUWtaVlUwTkJWRVZFVWtWRFNWQkpSVTVVU1VRbEpRPT0iOiI1ZDNlNWYzNSIsIkpTVlBRa1pWVTBOQlZFVkVUVUZKVEVsT1IxTkZUa1JKUkNVbCI6IjI5YmI3NDgxIn0%3D/?g=v87XQ8Qot1BdKB-_uknky5A "https://act.314actionfund.org/page/oneclickunsubscribe/5d3e5f35/29bb7481?utm_medium=email&utm_source=naughton&utm_content=2+-+here&utm_campaign=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&source=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&refcode=314_EM_FR_200603_3MO-3MC-3M5xAT_1M2xOP-1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr") to unsubscribe.
| Hi Friends,

My name is Dr. Cameron Webb, and I am a doctor, lawyer and educator running for Congress in Virginia's 5th Congressional District. I know you've read some emails from folks talking about our campaign, but I want to reach out personally.

Please let me explain why today is so important for our campaign -- [but if you're ready to help me become the first Black physician to be a voting member in Congress, please contribute now before our final FEC deadline tonight.](https://act.314actionfund.org/page/m/29bb7481/4bd7760e/5d3e5f35/77ae4b0c/1097093776/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IiIsImUzdGhjMnQ5ZlE9PSI6IjEwIn0%3D/?g=v87XQ8Qot1BdKB-_uknky5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/314_webb_junesplit?amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=naughton&utm_content=3+-+but+if+yoursquore+ready+to+help+me+becom&utm_campaign=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&source=314_EM_FR_200603_1M2xOP_1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr&refcode=314_EM_FR_200603_3MO-3MC-3M5xAT_1M2xOP-1M5XAT-3MC_STD_2_webbfr") | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, will be processed immediately when you click a button below:

Since day one of this campaign, we've been sincere and unapologetic about our focus on fixing healthcare and pressing for social justice. The crises of the past few months -- our global health pandemic and continued police-involved killings leading to nationwide demonstrations against racial injustice -- have only magnified the critical importance and timeliness of our message.

**It 's not lost on me that, if elected, I would be the first Black physician to be a voting member of the United States Congress. In fact, in moments like these, I'm more motivated than ever to bring a new voice and insights to the halls of Congress.**

As a practicing physician, I understand that access to quality, affordable healthcare is one of the defining issues of our time. Even during this campaign, I still work as a hospitalist -- a frontline provider -- taking care of COVID-19 patients on the wards at the University of Virginia. Every day in the hospital, I witness the critical need to reform our nation's healthcare system.

Our campaign continues to gather momentum because folks in our district understand that too. **Yesterday was the best fundraising day of our campaign!**

314 Action is a grassroots community of over 2.1 million people working to elect Democratic scientists who will use science and facts to address some of our most pressing issues like climate change and health care. In 2018 we played a pivotal role in flipping the US House of Representatives by electing 9 Democratic scientists to the House and Senate as well as over 100 to key legislative districts while raising over $5.2 million with an average donation of just $27.55. Now we're focused on flipping the US Senate, defeating 25 climate deniers in Congress and flipping key state legislatures across the country.
